_comment: Authoritative external baseline for context/comparison against the news-corpus dashboard. Figures taken from the Global CCS Institute 'Global Status of CCS 2025' report (executive-summary graphic + official press release), retrieved 2026-07-08. Global totals are triple-confirmed (report graphic + GCCSI press release + JPT/SPE). Regional entries are quoted from the report's 'Regional Spotlight'; some are operating facts, some are 2030/2050 targets, as labelled. Per-region operating capacity (Mtpa) is NOT hard-coded because it could not be verified to a primary source at build time — add it here once confirmed against the full report PDF.
source: Global CCS Institute — Global Status of CCS 2025 (“Staying the Course”)

regions.gccsi: US 45Q + Canada's ITC underpin momentum. The US has 39 operating facilities and has stored >223 Mt CO₂ cumulatively, with a strong pipeline (notably Louisiana & Texas). Federal uncertainty in the US has introduced headwinds.

5 projects operational + 7 more reached FID / financial close since mid-2024 (incl. Net Zero Teesside, Northern Endurance Partnership); Brevik (world's largest CCS cement plant) online June 2025. Operating capacity <3 Mtpa today → target >90 Mtpa by 2030. EU NZIA mandates 50 Mtpa CO₂ storage by 2030.
Jubail hub (9 Mtpa) and Yanbu hub (2 Mtpa) advancing; Oman, Qatar, UAE and — in Africa — Nigeria, Kenya and South Africa advancing pilot projects and early-stage regulatory frameworks.
Advancing as a leader in fossil-fuel decarbonisation; Huaneng's 1.5 Mtpa facility at the world's largest CCS-equipped coal-fired plant. The 15th Five-Year Plan is set to formally incorporate CCS.

A second facility came online in 2025; Gorgon and Moomba operating; Kasawari, Tangguh and Arthit in construction; 54 facilities across APAC at development stages. Regulatory progress in Australia, Indonesia and Malaysia.
Government targeting up to 240 Mtpa of CO₂ storage by 2050; 9 Advanced CCS Projects selected; CCS Business Act (2024) establishes the licensing framework. (Corpus groups Japan under APAC.)
Brazil advancing supportive incentives; Petrobras pre-salt CO₂ reinjection is among the largest single operating CCS operations globally.

caveat: GCCSI figures are the authoritative global project-pipeline baseline (operating + announced), on a facility/capacity (Mtpa) lens using GCCSI's own regional groupings — NOT this dashboard's recent-news-flow A$ lens. Use them to sanity-check what the corpus is and isn't catching. Regional highlights are quoted from the report's Regional Spotlight.
